mission.title: Install and Configure Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP)
mission.description: Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) is a comprehensive Kubernetes distribution that provides a robust platform for deploying and managing containerized applications. It includes features such as developer tools, CI/CD pipelines, and enhanced security. Key trade-offs include the complexity of installation and management compared to simpler Kubernetes distributions.

steps.title: Install the OpenShift CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the OpenShift CLI (oc) which is essential for interacting with your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/openshift/okd/releases/download/v4.17.0/openshift-client-linux-4.17.0.tar.gz

tar -xvf openshift-client-linux-4.17.0.tar.gz

sudo mv oc /usr/local/bin/

sudo mv odo /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the OpenShift CLI for Linux.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the oc binary to your PATH.
> Move the odo binary to your PATH.

steps.title: Create an OpenShift Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new OpenShift cluster using the OpenShift installer.

```bash

curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/openshift-install-linux.tar.gz

tar -xvf openshift-install-linux.tar.gz

sudo mv openshift-install /usr/local/bin/

openshift-install create cluster --dir=mycluster --log-level=info

```

> Download the OpenShift installer.
> Extract the installer.
> Move the installer to your PATH.
> Create a new cluster in the specified directory.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to access the newly created OpenShift cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(pwd)/mycluster/auth/kubeconfig

oc login -u kubeadmin -p $(cat mycluster/auth/kubeadmin-password)

```

> Set the KUBECONFIG environment variable.
> Log in to the OpenShift cluster using the kubeadmin credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your OpenShift cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

oc get nodes

oc get pods --all-namespaces

```

> List all nodes in the cluster and their statuses.
> Verify that all system pods are running.

steps.title: Post-install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures additional features like monitoring and autoscaling.

```bash

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-monitoring-operator/master/deploy/cluster-monitoring-operator.yaml

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-autoscaler/master/deploy/cluster-autoscaler-operator.yaml

```

> Install the monitoring operator.
> Install the cluster autoscaler.

uninstall.title: Remove the OpenShift Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the OpenShift cluster and all associated resources. Data loss may occur.

```bash

openshift-install destroy cluster --dir=mycluster --log-level=info

```

> Destroy the cluster and clean up resources.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Local Files
uninstall.description:
This step removes local files related to the OpenShift installation.

```bash

rm -rf mycluster

```

> Delete the cluster directory.

upgrade.title: Backup Cluster Resources
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your cluster resources to prevent data loss.

```bash

oc get all --all-namespaces -o yaml > backup.yaml

```

> Backup all resources to a YAML file.

upgrade.title: Upgrade OpenShift Cluster
upgrade.description:
This step upgrades the OpenShift cluster to the specified version.

```bash

openshift-install upgrade cluster --dir=mycluster --log-level=info

```

> Run the upgrade command.

upgrade.title: Verify Upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the nodes and pods after the upgrade.

```bash

oc get nodes

oc get pods --all-namespaces

```

> Ensure all nodes are in a Ready state.
> Verify that all pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with the kubeconfig or the cluster's certificate.

**Fix:**
Ensure the KUBECONFIG is set correctly and that the cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no nodes available in cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster nodes are not in a Ready state.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the nodes with 'oc get nodes' and troubleshoot any issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'oc login failed: Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the credentials used for login are incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the kubeadmin password in 'mycluster/auth/kubeadmin-password'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with image pull permissions or the image not being available.

**Fix:**
Check the image repository permissions and ensure the image exists.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new storage classes, improved monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Ensure all custom resources are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'oc get nodes' showing all nodes in a Ready state, and 'oc get pods --all-namespaces' should show all system pods running.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
